name: Buddy Greco
abstract: Armando Joseph "Buddy" Greco (August 14, 1926 – January 10, 2017) was an American jazz and pop singer and pianist who had a long career in the US and UK. His recordings have sold millions, including "Oh Look A-There Ain't She Pretty", "Up, Up and Away", and "Around the World".

concerts.label: Orange Colored Sky: Celebrating the Music of Nat King Cole
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Igoe
about_quotes.evidence: So Buddy Greco was the piano player and Benny, and bass player was a fellow named Clyde Lombardi. And so we played with just a small group for about an hour.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Igoe interviewed by Monk Rowe, Emerson, New Jersey, July 31, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: And it was a show that Buddy Rich had with Buddy Greco. It was called “Away We Go.” And it was 13 weeks, we were all in town for 13 weeks, and while I was there doing that T.V. show I met a lot of the musicians in L.A.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: he would take me out playing golf when people would invite him to the country club, so I’d go out and play golf with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: when I played at Basin Street East I met Al Hirt, ...(?)..., Trini Lopez, Buddy Greco, Maynard Ferguson.
